description_long: This object is a naturalistic wooden pole resembling a tree branch, featuring a main thick shaft with two smaller offshoots extending at angles. The bark texture is rough and dark brown, with visible cut ends that reveal a lighter, wood-colored interior. One of the smaller branches supports a single green leaf, adding a touch of organic detail. The pole is irregular in shape, with slight bends and natural imperfections typical of a real branch. The ends are cleanly cut, suggesting it has been trimmed or pruned. This object would blend seamlessly into park or garden settings, or serve as a decorative or functional element in urban landscaping.
description: A rough, dark brown wooden pole with branch offshoots and a single green leaf, featuring cut ends and natural bark texture.
description_view_0: The main shaft and two offshoot branches are clearly visible, with the green leaf attached to the right offshoot.
description_view_1: The cut ends of the main shaft and both offshoots are prominent, and the green leaf is seen from above.
description_view_2: The pole's length and curvature are emphasized, with the offshoots and a hint of the green leaf visible underneath.
description_view_3: The pole appears elongated and narrow, with the offshoots less visible and the green leaf mostly hidden.
